issues.title: AI Copilot falls back to hardcoded onboarding placeholders instead of querying real project data
issues.description: When both AI service and cached insights are empty, the function generates static placeholder insights instead of querying real project tables (rfis, submittals, punch_list_items, schedule_activities, budget_line_items) to produce data-driven fallback insights. This means the copilot shows generic tips instead of actionable intelligence even when the project has real data.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function: BEFORE: When cachedInsights and budgetInsights are both empty, the function falls back to hardcoded onboarding placeholder insights without querying any project data tables. The comment says '// Query real project data before falling back to onboarding placeholders' but the actual implementation after it generates static content. AFTER: Before generating onboarding placeholders, query real project data: (1) Count open/overdue RFIs from 'rfis' table where status != 'closed' and due_date < now(), (2) Count open punch list items from 'punch_list_items' where status in ('open','in_progress'), (3) Count pending submittals from 'submittals' where status = 'pending', (4) Query budget_line_items for any lines where spent_to_date > current_budget. For each query that returns non-zero results, generate a real AIInsight with type 'risk' or 'action', severity based on count thresholds (>10 critical, >5 warning, else info), concrete title like 'X overdue RFIs need attention', description with the actual count, and source 'computed'. Only show onboarding placeholders if ALL queries return zero results. Wrap each query in try/catch so one failure doesn't block others. Return these computed insights with dataSource 'computed'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FloatingAIButton and CopilotPanel lack keyboard navigation and focus management
issues.description: The floating AI button uses onMouseDown/onMouseUp for press animation but has no onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space activation feedback. The button lacks role and focus-visible styling. When the copilot panel opens, focus is not trapped inside it, and Escape does not close it. Screen readers cannot discover the insight count badge.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: BEFORE: The button element uses onMouseDown/onMouseUp for scale animation but no keyboard equivalents. No focus-visible outline. The aria-label exists but the insight count badge (the span with insightCount) has no aria-live region so screen readers miss updates. No onKeyDown handler. AFTER: (1) Add onKeyDown handler that applies the same scale(0.95) transform on Enter/Space keydown and scale(1.08) on keyup. (2) Add a focus-visible outline style: 'outline: 2px solid #F47820, outlineOffset: 2px' using onFocus/onBlur state or CSS-in-JS check for :focus-visible. (3) Wrap the insight count badge span in a span with aria-live='polite' and role='status' so screen readers announce count changes. (4) Add tabIndex={0} explicitly (buttons have it by default but confirm). (5) Add role='button' explicitly for clarity. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel has no conversation persistence or history navigation
issues.description: The CopilotPanel manages conversation messages in local component state only. When the panel is closed and reopened, all conversation history is lost. There is no integration with the ai_conversations or ai_messages Supabase tables defined in the backend schema. Enterprise users need persistent conversation history for audit trails and cross-session continuity.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: BEFORE: Messages are managed via useMultiAgentChat hook which stores them in memory only. Closing the panel loses all history. No calls to ai_conversations or ai_messages tables. No conversation list or history sidebar. AFTER: (1) On panel mount, query supabase from('ai_conversations').select('id, conversation_topic, started_at, message_count').eq('project_id', projectId).eq('user_id', currentUserId).order('started_at', {ascending: false}).limit(10) to load recent conversations. Show them as a small scrollable list at the top of the panel when no active conversation is selected, with each item showing topic and message_count. (2) When user sends the first message in a new conversation, insert into ai_conversations with project_id, user_id, and conversation_topic derived from the first message (first 50 chars). Store the returned conversation id in state. (3) After each message exchange (user + assistant), insert both into ai_messages with conversation_id, role, and content. (4) When user clicks a past conversation from the list, load its messages from ai_messages and populate the chat. (5) Add a 'New Conversation' button that clears current messages and resets conversationId. Wrap all Supabase calls in try/catch; on error show a toast but don't block the UI. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel is not optimized for mobile field use with no bottom sheet behavior
issues.description: The CopilotPanel slides in from the right at 360px width on desktop and 100% on mobile. On mobile, it lacks bottom sheet gesture behavior (swipe down to dismiss), the input area does not account for the virtual keyboard pushing content up, touch targets for suggested prompts are too small, and there is no safe-area-inset-bottom padding for notched phones.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: BEFORE: On mobile (isMobile check exists), the panel takes full width but behaves identically to desktop. The drag handle div exists but has no swipe-to-dismiss functionality. The input area at the bottom has no padding for safe-area-inset-bottom. Suggested prompt buttons use small padding. AFTER: (1) Add paddingBottom: 'calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 16px)' to the input area container at the bottom of the panel. (2) Increase suggested prompt button minimum height to 44px and padding to '10px 16px' to meet Apple HIG touch targets. (3) Add a simple swipe-to-dismiss: track onTouchStart/onTouchMove/onTouchEnd on the drag handle div. If the user swipes down more than 100px, call the close function. Store startY in a ref, calculate deltaY in onTouchMove, and apply translateY transform during drag for visual feedback. On touchEnd, if deltaY > 100 close the panel, else animate back to 0. (4) When the panel is open on mobile, add overflow: 'hidden' to document.body to prevent background scrolling, and remove it on close. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AgentMentionInput silently fails on send errors with no retry or error state
issues.description: The handleSend function in AgentMentionInput calls onSend but wraps it in a try/catch that only sets isSending to false on error. There is no error state displayed to the user, no retry mechanism, no toast notification on failure, and the user's message text is cleared even if sending fails, losing their input.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: BEFORE: The handleSend function clears the input value immediately before calling onSend. If onSend throws, the catch block only sets isSending to false. The user's typed message is lost and they see no error feedback. AFTER: (1) Save the current message text in a local variable before clearing. (2) Wrap the onSend call in try/catch. On success, keep the input cleared. On error: restore the saved message text back into setValue, call toast.error('Failed to send message. Please try again.') using the existing toast import from sonner, and set isSending to false. (3) Add an error state: const [sendError, setSendError] = useState<string | null>(null). Set it in the catch block, clear it on successful send. (4) Below the textarea, if sendError is truthy, render a small red text span: fontSize 12px, color colors.statusCritical, showing the error message with a 'Retry' button that re-calls handleSend. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Project Morning Pulse
invented_features.description: Auto-generated one paragraph natural language summary of project health every morning at 6 AM, pushed as the first message in the AI Copilot when the superintendent opens the app. Synthesizes overnight weather changes, overdue items created since yesterday, schedule risk changes, and any safety incidents. No competitor does this. It replaces the 15 minute manual morning review.

competitive_intel: SiteSync's multi-agent AI architecture with domain-specific specialists (schedule, cost, safety, quality, compliance, document) plus generative UI components (charts, forms, approval cards, cost breakdowns) is significantly ahead of Procore Assist which is a single flat chatbot. The AG-UI protocol stream panel with human-in-the-loop approval is enterprise-grade and matches nothing in ACC or Fieldwire. However, the copilot still runs mostly on mock/placeholder data rather than real Supabase queries, and conversation persistence is missing, which makes it a demo rather than a production tool. Fixing data wiring and conversation history would put this 2+ years ahead of any competitor's AI offering.
summary: The Project Intelligence module has an impressively architected multi-agent AI system with 6 specialist agents, generative UI rendering (13 component types), batch action preview, and AG-UI protocol streaming. The visual polish of individual components is strong with consistent theme usage. However, the module scores poorly on data richness (insights fall back to hardcoded placeholders instead of querying real project data), enterprise readiness (no conversation persistence to ai_conversations/ai_messages tables), accessibility (missing keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA live regions on the floating button and panel), mobile optimization (no swipe-to-dismiss, no safe-area padding, small touch targets), and error handling (silent failures in the agent input with lost user messages). The 5 issues target the highest-impact gaps: wiring real project data into fallback insights, adding accessibility to the primary entry points, persisting conversations for audit trails, optimizing for field mobile use, and preventing silent message loss on errors.
